reasoning: The stock price has recently shown positive short-term momentum, bouncing from roughly $50.72 on August 6 to $53.38 on August 15. This recovery has been supported by positive macroeconomic news (favorable CPI inflation data) and favorable corporate announcements, including a new "Focus to Win" cost-cutting program and management confidence (the Board electing to receive retainers in stock). Because of this near-term upward momentum and a lack of immediate negative catalysts before August 24, the short-term probability is estimated slightly above 50%. As per the instructions, uncertainty must increase with the forecast horizon, so the probabilities progressively smoothly converge toward 0.50. Over the 3- and 6-month horizons, intermediate quarterly earnings releases (likely in October and January) will introduce unforecastable variance, justifying a return to a near random-walk baseline (0.50-0.51). Furthermore, the dividend payable in late August already had its ex-dividend date on July 31, ensuring no artificial price drop before the September date, though subsequent ex-dividend dates in late October/early November provide a very minor downward technical pricing pressure for the later resolution dates.
